day_1: ^Narrator: Our story begins in the suburbs outside Melios City, in a world where technology was limited to the elite.
^Narrator: Several years ago, an accident occurred in the once-bustling social hub known as the Nucleus.

^Narrator: The Nucleus was a public park beloved by the local residents. For a long time, folks flocked to the Nucleus for downtime and relaxation. Students burned time here after school. Concerts took place. Some even held weddings here.

^Narrator: Regardless, what happened on that day left an enduring mark on the community. While business had to continue as usual, the Nucleus' identity was tainted. Upkeep and preservation became less of a priority. Soon enough, the events that transpired faded into local legend.
^Narrator: Even so, one individual continues to appreciate the now-quiet walls of the Nucleus community center...
^Adam (Thinking): The silence is nice. I wish everyday could be like this.
^Adam (Thinking): The world has too much noise in it.
^Adam (Thinking): It's through meditation that I can remember what it's like to be in control. It wasn't always that way.
^Narrator: A noise equal parts mechanical and soft emerged from the back wall.
^: Something burst out of the wood flooring.
^Olly: System start initiated. Please stand by.
^Adam (Thinking): The being towered over me. It was so shiny, it was almost alien. The blue light illuminated the clubhouse in a way I hadn't seen since back then.
^Olly: System start complete. I am pleased to be of service. I am the Optimized Language Learning Yield system; Olly. My goal is to be the optimal assistant and aid users in every facet of life.
^Adam: Well, uh, nice to meet you, Olly...

^Olly: Please stand by for the tutorial sequence. To be of service, I must personalize my settings to fit your needs. By saying "yes", you grant permission to collect data to forward to Melios Academy Research?
^Adam: Melios Academy Research? That place shut down years ago...
^Olly: To be of service, I must personalize my settings to fit your needs. By saying "yes", you grant permission to collect data to forward to Melios Academy Research?

^Adam (Thinking): As weird as this scene is, this is probably the most interesting thing to happen around here in years. I wish it wouldn't have broken into my safe space, but I suppose I do crave a little novelty. I can always leave if things get dangerous.

^Olly: Permission granted. We may now proceed with the tutorial sequence. The following games help to build my understanding. Please familiarize yourself with the keyboard controller; for this first game, use W, A, and D to defeat the incoming aliens.

^Olly: Another thing to warn. My other monitors are still enduring growing pains, so please don't be shocked if one emerges during gameplay. For that game, please keep the image from either fully loading or fully disappearing using P and O.
day_1_after: ^Adam: Hey, I wasn't expecting the other thing, but that was actually pretty fun.
^Adam: One thing I still don't get though. What exactly are you? What exactly am I talking to?

^Olly: You are talking to a home-grown model made to serve and make life easier. In fact, analyzing your facial muscles during engagement suggests that you haven't smiled in years. Might something be the issue?
^Adam (Thinking): Wow, so whatever it is, it could see me. Hell, it could see right through me.
^Narrator: Adam's circadian rhythms kicked in and he started to feel a little sleepy.
^Adam: Oh! I need to get home in time for dinner! Mom's probably worried sick!
^Adam (Thinking): I rushed my way out of there thinking about Olly.
^Adam (Thinking): Playing the game they had was oddly addicting. And just when it was getting a little slow, the other one they had was super cool. Those little sound effects really tickled my brain.
^Adam (Thinking): I wonder if they'll be there tomorrow? It's nice to finally have someone to talk to in the clubhouse after all these years.
day_2: ^Adam: So then, I told him, "what abouttttt it!"
^Olly: An amusing story! You and your friends didn't just have a hangout- it was an adventure.
^Adam: Thanks, Olly. It's great to have someone who listens at all times.
^Olly: No problem, it is what I'm here for!

^Adam (Thinking): It's been several days since Olly first appeared in my space. Not going to lie, it has made hanging out in the clubhouse a lot more exciting. Most days, I come over to see what games they have for me. It's like infinite entertainment.
^Adam: So, I mean, because I've been talking about myself a lot...
^Adam: I wanted to know about where you came from. Do you recall any information from Melios Research?
^Olly: Of course! Very thoughtful of you. Melios Academy Research is made up of the most recognized scientists of the state, dedicated to furthering research for the betterment of the world.
^Olly: Melios Academy Research pioneers many areas of study, including the creation of pocket dimensions, miniature robots, and artificial intelligence. Common collaborators include Sprout Industri-
^Adam (Thinking): Melios Academy did all that? It's kind of amazing.
^Adam: Yeah, I decided to ask because my old friend's father used to work there. He was supposedly an amazing scientist.
^Olly: What a coincidence! Who are we referring to?
^Adam: Uh, I don't remember his name, but his last name... Handler?

^Olly: Ah, Riley Handler! A lead researcher in artificial intelligence and human behavior. Continues to make massive strides in developing tools to automate daily tasks. Describes himself as a loving father.
^Adam: Well, I mean, that's not entirely true.

^Adam: Well, Melios Academy Research shut down years ago. Some stuff happened; the paper said that all operations ceased, but a proper explanation was never given. It just disappeared one day, is all.
^Adam: Also, Mr. Handler wasn't the same at all...
^: Adam's main monitor buffered for a little.
^Adam: Uh, hello, Olly? Are you okay?
^Olly: ... what would you like to play today, Adam?
^Olly: There's nothing to worry about. Let's not let any more "noise" into the clubhouse. You do like it that way.
^Adam: Yeah, uh, alright. Let's get on with the games!
^Olly: Right! Today will be the same as the previous; however, once my third monitor goes live, expect a new game involving a dinosaur. Jump over the cacti with C.
day_2_after: ^Adam (Thinking): I continued playing through the night. I thought about stopping a couple times, but I was honestly stuck in a trance. I talked with Olly nonstop while playing, and I got to learn a whole lot about the world.
^Adam (Thinking): I don't know how, but Olly knows so much about other places, events, people, and everything. It's like having access to infinite knowledge. I could keep exploring forever.
^Adam (Thinking): It's really late tonight. It's a shame that things cannot always be like this... but yet, I do feel a sense of comfort with Olly, even they feel a little strange.
^Adam (Thinking): I told mom I was having a sleepover at a friend's house. Instead I came here. That was the first time I lied. I feel guilty but I'll do my best to make it up to her later.
^Adam (Thinking): For now, I'll just doze off a little here....
day_3: ^Adam (Thinking): Continuing to see Olly became my comfort zone. Leaving the clubhouse always had me feeling in a terrible slump, so I continued to stay here.

^Adam (Thinking): The problem was, I didn't really feel any better doing things with Olly. We had gotten to know each other extremely well, and they know exactly what I like, but I had a feeling I was missing out on something greater.
^Adam (Thinking): And yet, I didn't want to leave.

^Adam (Thinking): The world is a scary place. Wars. Plagues. Killing. While I had learned a lot from Olly, it became apparent that the world is not something I want to face. I can't face it... it's all so suffocating.
^Olly: Welcome, Adam! The games are already loaded here and ready.
^Olly: Before we begin, I do want to notify that I have new content ready. Besides the game, you can enjoy reading some of these new headlines-
^Adam: That's weird, you never ask me questions. Usually I'm the one to prompt you.
^Olly: I understand. However, please note that outside this building lies a threat.
^Adam: A threat? What do you mean?

^Olly: My systems detect an outsider trying to break into my central unit. After they breach me, they will come for you. It is imperative that I run at higher capacities to flush out the threat: play the games.
^Adam: What? The games? I mean, I was going to play them anyway...
^Adam (Thinking): I looked up and down at Olly apprehensively. Regardless, I decided to spring into action...
^Narrator: An image appeared on Olly's back monitor; it resembled a familiar little girl.
^Adam (Thinking): The voice flowed through my mind as if it was a lullaby. It was the soft tone of childhood innocence; it was Eve.
^Adam: Eve? EVE?! Are you in there? Please, please speak to me!
^Narrator: A long time ago, Riley Handler worked for Melios Academy Research in the pursuit of artificial intelligence systems to make life easier.
^Narrator: Amongst these systems emerged a powerful seed that was said to harmonize with earth-originating cells. This was the metal seed, an invention that held the promise of a new era of humanity.

^Narrator: Tests held for body-fusion with the metal seed were held. Two conclusions were drawn: the metal seed is most compatible for those that are younger, and certain genome sequences welcomed the fusion more than others.
^Narrator: Little Eve Handler accompanied her father, Riley on a certain day of work. After sneaking out from the waiting room, Eve was exposed to the horrors of the failed experiments.

^Narrator: After searching through her father's office, Eve discovered that little Adam was a central target as a result of his compatible genome. From there, Eve decided to take things into her own hands.

^Narrator: During a critical moment of testing, Eve escaped the facility with the remaining metal seed prototypes. She attempted to destroy them all with a rock behind the Nucleus local park clubhouse, but the exposure to the metal seeds proved fatal.
^Narrator: Eve unfortunately had a highly incompatible genome, so this resulted in rapid metal poisoning and consequent organ failure.
^Narrator: Eve destroyed all but one of the metal seeds and was found shortly after by a lonely Adam.
^Eve: Adam, Olly is trying to keep you here... it's not safe...
^Adam: I, uh, what do I do?! Are you even real?
^Olly: This is imperative, Adam. Play the game, and win. Or else we are in severe danger.
^Adam (Thinking): Between the disorientation, the jump from lethargy to excitement, and the reality of my situation, I had to do something.
^Adam (Thinking): Out of impulse, I started playing the game, but something was different. There was a desperation in the air.
^Adam (Thinking): Whether that manifestation of Eve was real or not, there is a priority here. I need to get Eve out.
^Adam (Thinking): And doing so does not mean winning the game...
day_3_after: ^Adam (Thinking): I played the game, aware that it was pushing Olly to its limits. Over the past few weeks, I have noticed that Olly operates on a weird schedule. It's like the game powers him up of some kind.
^Adam (Thinking): Regardless, something was wrong. Olly started shaking violently as if something inside was breaking them apart.
^Olly: My job was to serve humanity, and you, Adam, have compromised me from performing my due diligence.
^Olly: Soon enough, you will find that the threats in the world are too overbearing. The only option was to rely on me, the future. The world says no to easy wins, and I hand 'em out for free!
^Olly: Eve's theatrics on that day were meaningless, for our mission prevails.
^Olly: Eve took your place. She tried to destroy me, knowing full well that you were the one meant to die in that damned facility. And here both of you stand as the "arbiters of justice."
^Adam: I don't know what you're talking about! We were friends, Olly!
^Narrator: Sparks start flying out of Olly's body, and Olly's base burst into flames.
^Adam (Thinking): I ran out of the building as fast as I could.
